design_philosophy: A command-center for deep learning. Precision, high-density information, and brutalist clarity. The grid is visible. No decorative gradients; color is used purely to signal system state and hierarchy.

spacing_and_layout.surface_style: Grid Borders (Technical Look). 1px solid borders, no shadows.

layout_structure.main_grid: grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-4 h-screen w-full bg-zinc-950 text-zinc-50 border-collapse overflow-hidden
layout_structure.left_sidebar: col-span-1 border-r border-zinc-800 flex flex-col h-full bg-zinc-950
layout_structure.code_area: col-span-2 border-r border-zinc-800 flex flex-col h-full relative

code_editor.linter_style: Custom Monaco squiggly lines via decorations. Errors shown in a bottom diagnostic panel.

charts.style: Minimalist. No grid lines behind the chart, 1px solid line for data. Tooltip must be a sharp rectangle with 1px border. Green (#10B981) for Accuracy, Red (#EF4444) for Loss.

ai_assistant.style: Chat bubbles should be flat rectangles. AI responses have a subtle left border (accent_ai: #8B5CF6). User messages are aligned right with surface background. Input field should be borderless but sit inside a 1px border container.

websocket_feedback.style: Terminal-like output. Monospace font, tiny text. Alternating row colors (zinc-900/zinc-950) for readability. Auto-scrolls to bottom. Agent status prominently displayed with a glowing dot.

buttons.primary: bg-primary text-white hover:bg-primary_hover font-mono uppercase text-xs tracking-[0.1em] px-6 py-3 transition-colors border border-transparent
buttons.secondary: bg-transparent text-zinc-300 hover:text-white border border-zinc-700 hover:border-zinc-500 font-mono uppercase text-xs tracking-[0.1em] px-4 py-2 transition-colors
buttons.danger: bg-transparent text-red-500 hover:bg-red-500/10 border border-red-500/50 hover:border-red-500 font-mono uppercase text-xs tracking-[0.1em] px-4 py-2 transition-colors

cards.default: bg-zinc-900/50 border border-zinc-800 p-4 relative overflow-hidden
cards.metric: bg-zinc-900 border border-zinc-800 p-4 flex flex-col justify-between

interactions_and_motion.hover_states: Hard background color swaps or border color brightness shifts. No soft fades.
interactions_and_motion.loading: ASCII spinners or sharp progress bars (1px height) at the top of containers.

icons.style: Stroke width 1.5, size 16px to 20px, sharp and functional.

images.description: Abstract neural network data flow used for AI agent idle state or background texture
Developer portrait for user profile or agent avatar

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Ensure > 4.5:1 ratio across all text. Red (#EF4444) on Dark (#09090B) passes, but be careful with thin fonts.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. This is a technical dashboard. Do NOT use soft shadows (`shadow-lg`, etc) or large border radii (`rounded-xl`). Use `rounded-none` or `rounded-sm` at most.
2. The layout should occupy the full viewport height (`h-screen`). Use a 3-column or 4-column CSS grid where the Monaco Editor takes the most space.
3. Implement the real-time feedback panel as a streaming terminal. Use WebSockets to simulate or receive data.
4. For the charts, use `recharts` with a dark theme. Remove all grid lines (`<CartesianGrid vertical={false} horizontal={false} />`).
5. Use `JetBrains Mono` for headers and code, `IBM Plex Sans` for standard text. Import them via Google Fonts.
6. Do not use standard Shadcn Cards. Use flat `div` elements with `border border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-950/50` to maintain the 'Grid Borders' brutalist look.
7. Include Play, Stop, and Step buttons for the Model Training controls.
8. Use the Lucide-React icons heavily for toolbars.
9. The AI Assistant chat should look like an integrated terminal pane, not a floating widget.
